33.text: Title This Law may be cited as the “Foundations Law 2018” or “this Law”.

34.text: Legislative authority This Law is made by the Ruler.

36.text: This Law applies in the jurisdiction of the Dubai International Financial Centre.

41.text: This Law is enacted on the date specified in the enactment notice in respect of this Law.

43.text: This Law comes into force on the date specified in the enactment notice in respect of this Law.

58.text: PART 2: NATURE OF A DIFC FOUNDATION

60.text:
(1) A Foundation is a body corporate with a legal personality separate from that of its Founder(s) and any other person.   
(2) A Foundation has the capacity, rights and privileges of a natural person...

62.text: (1) A Foundation may, but need not, be established for a fixed period or for a specified limited period, provided that it may be dissolved at an earlier time in accordance with the provisions of this ...

63.text: (2) If a Foundation is to be wound up and dissolved upon:

64.text:
(a) the happening of some event; or   
(b) the expiration of a fixed period of time,   
(c) the details of the event or period must be specified in its By-laws.

65.text: (3) If a person is to have the right to wind up and dissolve a Foundation, the details of such right must be specified in its By-laws, subject to any right arising under any applicable law.

68.text: (a) must be certain, reasonable and possible; and (b) must not be unlawful or contrary to public policy in the DIFC.

69.text: (2) A Foundation may be established for:

70.text: (a) objects which are exclusively charitable; or (b) one or more of the following:

71.text: (i) objects which are not exclusively charitable; or (ii) objects to benefit persons by name, category or class.

72.text: (3) It is sufficient, for purposes of Article 12(2)(b)(i), for the Charter to provide that a Foundation is to hold property selected in accordance with its By-laws.

73.text: (4) It is sufficient, for purposes of Article 12(2)(b)(ii), for the Charter to provide that a person or class of persons to receive benefits may be determined in accordance with the By-laws.

74.text: (5) A Foundation may not carry out any commercial activities, except those necessary for, and ancillary or incidental to, its objects.

75.text: (6) The objects of a Foundation may only be amended:

76.text: (a) where there is an express power to do so in the Charter; or (b) by order of the Court.

77.text: (7) If there is no express power to amend the objects in a Foundation’s Charter then, subject to Articles 12(1) and 12(2), an object may be amended by order of the Court on an application made by or o...

79.text: (i) suitable; or (ii) practicable in administering the Foundation;

80.text: (e) in the case of a charitable object, the object has ceased to be charitable (by being useless or harmful to the community or otherwise); or

81.text: the object has ceased in any other way to provide a suitable and effective method of using the property of the Foundation,

89.text: (b) any aspect of the validity of the disposition or the interpretation or effect thereof;

113.text: PART 3: ESTABLISHMENT OF A DIFC FOUNDATION

115.text: (1) The Founder(s) may apply for the establishment of a Foundation by signing and filing with the Registrar an application for its establishment in the manner prescribed by the Registrar.

116.text: (2) The application filed with the Registrar under Article 17(1) shall be signed by each Founder and shall include:

118.text: (3) A Foundation shall not use a name which does not comply with the requirements prescribed in the Operating Law.

119.text: 4) A person may not be named as a Registered Agent unless that person is a Qualified Person.

120.text: ) Upon lodgement of the application and payment of the prescribed fee, the Registrar shall either:

121.text:
(a) register the Foundation;   
(b) seek further information in respect of the proposed Foundation; or (c) refuse the application.

122.text: (6) On the registration of a Foundation, the Registrar shall:

123.text:
(a) issue a certificate that the Foundation is established and such certificate shall expressly set out its status as a Foundation;   
(b) assign to the Foundation a number, which shall be the Foundat...

124.text: (7) A Foundation shall hold a Licence pursuant to Article 9 of the Operating Law.

126.text: The constitution of a Foundation comprises:

127.text: (a) its Charter, and (b) subject to Article 20(4), its By-laws.

129.text: (1) A Foundation must have a Charter which shall be in the English language.

130.text: (2) The Charter must contain the following:

132.text: (3) The Charter may contain any matter:

133.text:
(a) that is otherwise required or permitted to be in the By-laws in accordance with Article 20; or   
(b) that the Founder(s) think fit,   
(i) provided that there is no provision which is contrary to...

134.text: (4) The Charter may be amended only if:

135.text: (a) the Charter so provides; or (b) the Court so orders under Article 41.

136.text: (5) Where a Charter specifies an exclusively charitable object, it may not provide for the amendment of the Charter so as to specify a non-charitable object in place of the charitable object.

137.text: (6) Each Founder must subscribe to the Charter, if a Founder is:

138.text: (a) an individual, by signing the Charter in his own name; or (b) a body corporate, by an authorised signatory signing the Charter.

139.text: (7) The Board of Directors of the DIFCA may prescribe in the Regulations model provisions to be known as the “Standard Charter”, and a Foundation may, for its Charter, adopt the whole or any part ther...

140.text: (8) If a Foundation proposes to amend its Charter, it must after completing its internal processes necessary to amend the Charter, lodge the amended Charter with the Registrar within a period of thirt...

141.text: (9) The amended Charter shall come into force upon it being registered by the Registrar, and, if the change involves a change of name, upon the issue of a certificate of name change by the Registrar.

142.text: (10) The Charter may provide that if at any time the property of the Foundation includes any property which by reason of the law of the UAE or any other specified jurisdiction may be held only by a na...

143.text: Foundation) shall have effect, and the Charter may further provide that such provision may not be amended or revoked.

144.text: (11) If the Charter contains a provision of the type referred to in Article 19(10) then notwithstanding any other provision of this Law, that provision may not be amended or revoked.

166.text: (3) A Founder or a body corporate may be appointed as a member of the Council.

188.text: (a) that it is in the best interests of the Foundation; and (b) that the Council has acted, or will be acting, in good faith.

193.text: (1) A Foundation may, but need not, have a Registered Agent.

194.text: (2) The Registered Agent of a Foundation:

195.text: (a) must be appointed in accordance with this Law; and (b) has, in respect of the Foundation, the functions specified in this Law and the By-laws.

196.text: (3) On the establishment of a Foundation, the proposed Registered Agent named under Article 17(2)(e) becomes the Registered Agent of the Foundation.

197.text: (4) Details of any change of the Registered Agent must be provided to the Registrar within thirty (30) days of the occurrence of the change. A Foundation which fails to comply with this requirement is...

198.text: (5) A Foundation may not have more than one Registered Agent at any time.

199.text: 25. Liability of Council members and others

200.text: (1) This Article applies to a person appointed under the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation when acting or purporting to be acting in the course of that appointment.

202.text: (a) in the Charter or By-laws; or (b) in a contract between the Foundation and a person to whom this Article applies,

203.text: relieves, releases or excuses that person from any liability for fraud, wilful misconduct or gross negligence.

204.text: (3) Any insurance purchased and maintained by the Foundation in respect of a person to whom this Article applies must not include insurance in respect of any liability the person may incur:

205.text: (b) to pay a fine in respect of a contravention of this Law; or

206.text: (c) any costs the person may incur:

207.text: (i) in defending criminal proceedings in which the person is convicted; or (ii) in defending civil proceedings brought by the Foundation in which judgment is given against the person.

208.text: 26. Reservation to Founder of powers to amend, revoke, vary or terminate

209.text: (1) Subject to Article 26(2), a Founder may not reserve to himself any powers to amend, revoke or vary the Charter or By-laws or to terminate the Foundation.

210.text: (2) A Founder may reserve the following powers:

211.text:
(a) a power to amend, revoke or vary the terms of the Charter or By-laws, or both of them, in whole or in part;   
(b) subject to Article 12(2), a power to amend, revoke or vary the objects of the Fou...

212.text: provided that the power to amend, revoke, vary or terminate, as the case may be, is detailed in full in the Charter, and provided that these powers are only reserved:

213.text: (d) for a period not exceeding the duration of a Founder’s life, if he is a natural person; or (e) for a period not exceeding fifty (50) years from the date of establishment of the Foundation, if the ...

214.text: and thereafter any such powers so reserved shall lapse, notwithstanding the terms of the Charter.

215.text: (3) If, at any time in respect of a Foundation:

216.text: (a) any power has been reserved or granted under Article 26(1) to more than one Founder; and (b) more than one Founder is capable of exercising it at that time,

217.text: then such power must be exercised by those Founders unanimously unless the Charter provides otherwise.

220.text: (a) to accept or recognise only demands or requests, or the effects of any approval, veto, or compelled action or the exercise of any power, which are given by or are the result of persons acting of t...

221.text: act, or exercise the power is not a person either appointed or so authorised pursuant to this Law, the Charter or the By-laws of the Foundation.

240.text: 31. Limitation to the transferability of rights in respect of a Foundation

241.text: (1) Unless otherwise provided in the By-laws, the right to receive payments from a Foundation are transferable.

242.text: (2) The By-laws may limit the right to transfer a right to payment in one (1) or more of the following cases:

245.text: 32. Conduct of business in the DIFC

246.text: A Foundation shall, comply with the requirements of this Law and Part 3 of the Operating Law in respect to the conduct of its business in the DIFC.

248.text: (1) Unless inconsistent with this Article 33, Articles 122 to 124 of the Companies Law shall apply to a Foundation, as if it were a Company and any reference to “Director(s)” or “Officer(s)” in releva...

249.text: (2) A Foundation’s accounts shall be approved by the Council and signed on their behalf by at least two members of the Council. A Foundation which fails to comply with this requirement is liable to a ...

250.text: (3) A Foundation shall within thirty (30) days after the accounts have been approved by the Council:

251.text:
(a) if it has not appointed a Registered Agent, file a copy of its annual accounts with the Registrar; or   
(b) if it has appointed a Registered Agent, provide a copy of its annual accounts to its Re...

252.text: (4) A Foundation which fails to comply with the requirements in Article 33(3) is liable to a fine, as set out in Schedule 3.

253.text: (5) A person who makes a statement that is false, misleading or deceptive in a material way to the Registrar or the Registered Agent in respect of accounts, is liable to a fine as set out in Schedule ...

255.text: 34. Exercise of functions of the Registrar

256.text: (1) In the exercise of his functions under this Law, the Registrar has all the powers and duties conferred upon him by:

257.text: (a) Articles 6 and 7 of the Operating Law; and (b) Articles 22 to 33 of the Operating Law, including the power of delegation.

258.text: (2) The Registrar may enter into an arrangement with a Registered Agent under which:

259.text: (a) any document, form or fee required by this Law or the Relevant Laws to be lodged with or paid to the Registrar for the establishment or continuation of a Foundation in the DIFC, may be lodged with...

260.text: (b) the Registered Agent is required to perform some or all assessments, checks and verifications required under this Law or the Relevant Laws to ascertain and confirm whether an applicant or Foundati...

261.text: (i) adheres to all AML Requirements, the UBO Regulations and other requirements of the Relevant Laws; and (ii) maintains Accounting Records in accordance with Article 33,

262.text: and to provide a certification of such matters to the Registrar in an agreed format.

263.text: (3) The Registrar in the performance of any function under the Relevant Laws may in good faith rely, without further enquiry, upon the certification of a Registered Agent given under Article 34(2), pr...

265.text: (a) revoke any arrangement entered into with a Registered Agent under Article 34(2) if the Registrar is satisfied that the Registered Agent has acted in breach of its agreed obligations to the Registr...

266.text: (5) Where an applicant is represented by a Registered Agent in an application to establish or continue a Foundation, the Registrar shall be permitted to communicate directly with such Registered Agent...

267.text: 35. Register to be kept and made available for public inspection

268.text: (1) The Registrar must keep a Register for the purposes of this Law.

269.text: (2) The Register must include the information contained in Articles 17(2)(a) to (e), inclusive of the details of any changes filed with the Registrar (other than the nationality and address of any nat...

270.text: (3) The Registrar must, on payment of any prescribed fee, make the Register available for public inspection at any reasonable time.

271.text: (4) On payment of any prescribed fee, the Registrar must supply a person with a certificate stating whether or not a named body is a Foundation and, if it is, the following details as they appear in t...

273.text: (5) On payment of any prescribed fee, the Registrar must supply a Person with Sufficient Interest with a certified copy of the Charter as filed with the Register.

274.text: (6) The following Documents are admissible in evidence in legal proceedings:

275.text: (a) a certificate supplied by the Registrar under Article 35(4); or (b) a certified copy of the Charter supplied under Article 35(5).

277.text: (1) A record delivered to the Registrar under this Law may be kept by the Registrar in any form:

278.text: (a) that is approved by the Board of Directors of the DIFCA; and (b) that is capable of being reproduced in a legible form.

279.text: (2) The Registrar is to be taken to have complied with an obligation to keep a record if he has complied with Article 36(1) in respect of the record.

280.text: (3) The Registrar may destroy a record kept by the Registrar if:

281.text: (a) it is an original record and it has been recorded and kept in accordance with Article 36(1); or (b) it relates solely to a Foundation that has been dissolved more than six (6) years previously.

282.text: 37. Registrar may change registration number of Foundation

283.text: The Registrar may, for good cause, change the registration number of a Foundation, provided that he must inform the Foundation if he does so.

284.text: 38. Power of the Registrar to refuse Documents

285.text: (1) The Registrar may refuse to accept any Document delivered under this Law if:

287.text: PART 7: JUDICIAL AND NON JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS

288.text: 39. Role of the Court in administration of Foundations

289.text:
(1) The Court may intervene in the administration of a Foundation to the extent its jurisdiction is invoked by a Person with Sufficient Interest or as provided by Law.   
(2) A Foundation is not subje...

290.text: 40. Power of the Court to order compliance

291.text: (1) If the Court is satisfied that a person has failed to comply with:

292.text: (a) a requirement of this Law, the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation; or (b) an obligation imposed on the person by this Law, the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation, (d) the Court may order the perso...

293.text: (2) If the Court is satisfied that a Foundation, acting through its Council, has failed to carry out its objects or any of them, the Court may order the Foundation to do so.

294.text: An order under this Article may specify the action the person or Foundation is required to take.

295.text: 41. Power of the Court to order amendment of Charter or By-laws

296.text: (1) The Court may on an application made by or on behalf of a Founder, the Foundation or a Guardian order the amendment of the Charter or the By-laws of a Foundation, if the Court is satisfied:

297.text:
(a) that the change will assist the Foundation to administer its property or to attain its objects; or   
(b) that those objects are no longer attainable and that the change will assist the Foundation...

298.text: (2) If the order is to amend a Foundation’s Charter, the thirty (30) day period referred to Article 19(8) to file an amended Charter with the Registrar shall be calculated from the date of the Court o...

299.text: (3) Without limiting Article 41(1), the Court may make an order under Article 41(1) in the event that a Foundation does not have By-laws, or that the relevant provisions of the By-laws cannot be readi...

300.text: 42. Power of the Court to give directions

301.text: (1) This Article applies if the Court is satisfied:

302.text: (a) that if it gives a direction it will assist a Foundation to administer its property or to carry out its objects; or

303.text: (b) that it is otherwise desirable for the Court to give a direction.

304.text: (2) The Court may on an application made by or on behalf of a Founder, the Foundation or a Guardian give a direction as to:

305.text:
(a) the meaning and effect of a provision or term in the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation;   
(b) the manner in which the Council is required to carry out the administration of the Foundation’s prop...

306.text: (3) The Court may, in addition to giving a direction under Article 42(2), make such an order as it thinks fit to give effect to the direction.

307.text: 43. Power of the Court to protect interests under a Foundation

308.text: (1) The Court may in any proceeding under this Part by order appoint a person to protect the interests of a person who the Court is satisfied is a Qualified Recipient under a Foundation, where the Cou...

309.text: (2) A person so appointed may represent the person whose interests he has been appointed to protect in any dealing with the Foundation or in any proceeding under this Part.

310.text: (3) Where a representative has been appointed under Article 43(1), no settlement affecting a person to whom Article 43(1) applies shall be valid without the approval of the Court.

311.text: 44. Power of the Court to dismiss or appoint a Registered Agent

312.text: (1) If the Court is satisfied that it is in the interest of a Foundation to do so, it may order:

313.text: (a) the removal of its Registered Agent; and/or (b) the appointment of a Qualified Person to be its Registered Agent.

314.text: The Foundation must give notice of the change of Registered Agent to the Registrar within thirty (30) days of the order of the Court under Article 44(1). A Foundation which fails to comply with this r...

315.text: 45. Power of the Court to take action on behalf of others

316.text: (1) This Article applies where the Court is satisfied that a person has failed to comply with:

317.text: (a) a requirement of this Law, the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation; or (b) an obligation imposed on the person by this Law, the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation.

318.text: (2) The Court may, by order, require the Registrar comply with the requirement or obligation on behalf of the person who has failed to do so.

319.text: (3) The Court must not do so unless it is satisfied:

320.text:
(a) that to do so will assist the Foundation in the administration of its property or the attainment of its objects; or   
(b) that it is otherwise desirable that it should do so.

321.text: (4) Where the Court does so, its order has the same effect as if it were an action taken by the person required to comply with the requirement or obligation.

322.text: 46. General power of the Court in respect of orders

323.text:
(1) An order made by the Court under this Part in respect of a Foundation may, in particular, provide for the appointment or removal of a person appointed under its By-laws.   
(2) Any order made by t...

324.text: 47. Power to set aside a transfer or disposition of property due to mistake

326.text: (a) ‘person exercising a power’ means a person who exercises a power to transfer or make other disposition of property to a Foundation on behalf of a Founder and a person who exercises a power over a ...

329.text: (A) the effect of; (B) any consequences of; or (C) any of the advantages to be gained by, a transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundation, or the exercise of a power over or in relation to...

330.text: (ii) a mistake as to a fact existing either before or at the time of, a transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundation, or the exercise of a power over or in relation to a Foundation or pro...

331.text: (iii) a mistake of law including a law of a foreign jurisdiction; and references to a transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundation do not include a testamentary disposition.

332.text: The Court may on the application of any person specified in Article 49(1), and in the circumstances set out in Article 47(3), declare that a transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundation:

333.text:
(a) by a Founder or Contributor acting in person (whether alone or with any other Founder or Contributor); or   
(b) through or by reason of a person exercising a power,   
(e) or the exercise of a po...

334.text: (ii) is of no effect from the time of its exercise.

335.text: (3) The circumstances are where a Founder, Contributor or person exercising a power:

336.text:
(a) made a mistake in relation to the transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundation; and   
(b) would not have made that transfer or other disposition but for that mistake,   
(f) and the ...

337.text: 48. Power to set aside a transfer or disposition of property exercised by fiduciary power

338.text: (1) In this Article, ‘person exercising a power’ means a person who exercises a power to transfer or make other disposition of property to a Foundation on behalf of a Founder and who owes a fiduciary ...

339.text: (2) The Court may on the application of any person specified in Article 48(1), and in the circumstances set out in Article 48(3), declare that a transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundati...

340.text: (a) has such effect as the Court may determine; or (b) is of no effect from the time of its exercise.

341.text: (3) The circumstances referred to in Article 48(2) are where, in relation to the exercise of his or her power, the person exercising a power:

342.text:
(a) failed to take into account any relevant considerations or took into account irrelevant considerations; and   
(b) would not have exercised the power, or would not have exercised the power in the ...

343.text: (4) This Article applies whether or not the circumstances set out in Article 48(3) occurred as a result of any lack of care or other fault on the part of the person exercising a power, or on the part ...

344.text: 49. Applications and orders under Articles 47 and 48

345.text: (1) An application under Article 47(2) or 48(2) may be made by any Founder or Contributor or any of his or her personal representatives or successors in title, the Foundation or any person to whom pro...

346.text: (2) The Court may, consequential upon a declaration made under either of Articles 47 and 48, make such order as it thinks fit.

347.text: (3) Nothing in Articles 47 and 48 shall prejudice:

348.text: (a) any application for a declaration that a transfer or other disposition of property to a Foundation, or the exercise of any power over or in relation to a Foundation or property of a Foundation, is...

349.text: (b) any personal remedy which may be available against any person.

350.text: 50. Provisions for facilitating Foundation division or amalgamation

351.text: (1) This Article applies where the members of the Council of one or more Foundations unanimously resolve that the property of a Foundation should be divided amongst two (2) or more Foundations, or tha...

352.text: (2) An application may made to the Court under Article 50(1) for the division of the property of the Foundation, or the amalgamation of the Foundations, on notice to all Persons with Sufficient Intere...

353.text: (3) On any application under Article 50(1), the Court may make such orders as it considers appropriate to facilitate the division or amalgamation, including:

355.text: (4) The Court may take into consideration the difference in tax attributes and other pertinent factors in administering the property of any separate Foundation, in making applicable tax elections, and...

356.text: (5) A separate Foundation created pursuant to this Article 50 must be treated as a separate Foundation for all purposes from the date on which the order of the Court is made, including, but not limite...

357.text: (6) In dividing the property of a Foundation between two (2) or more separate Foundations, the Court shall accomplish the division by dividing the property on a fractional basis and transferring any p...

358.text:
(a) with a pro rata portion of each asset forming part of the property of the existing Foundation; or   
(b) on a non-pro rata basis based on either the fair market value of the assets on the date of ...

359.text: (7) Each of the Foundations affected by any order of the Court and any Foundation established by order of the Court shall give details of the change of status or establishment of the Foundations to th...

360.text: The Registrar shall make such entries in the Register as are required to give effect to the order of the Court and ensure any new or amalgamated Foundation is correctly registered.

362.text: (1) Where the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation provide that any dispute or Administration Question arising between any of the parties in relation to the Foundation shall be submitted to arbitration,...

363.text: (2) Where the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation do not provide that any dispute or Administration Question arising in relation to the Foundation shall be submitted to arbitration but the parties to t...

364.text: (3) The Arbitration Law shall apply to an arbitration under this Law in accordance with the provisions of Schedule 2.

365.text: (4) The Court may make such orders in relation to an arbitration or possible arbitration which supplement or vary the application of Schedule 2 as the Court deems appropriate in the circumstances.

367.text: (1) This Article shall apply except to the extent otherwise provided in the Charter or By-laws of a Foundation.

368.text: (2) The arbitral tribunal may, in addition to all other powers of the tribunal, at any stage in an arbitration under this Law, exercise all the powers of the Court (whether arising by law, including t...

369.text: The arbitral tribunal has the same powers to appoint a person to represent the interests of any person (including a minor, a person unborn or unascertained) or class of person in an arbitration concer...

370.text: 53. Provision of information by a Foundation

371.text: (1) A Person with Sufficient Interest in a Foundation may make written requests to the Foundation for full and accurate information in respect of any of the following:

373.text: (2) Subject to Article 53(4), a Foundation is not required to provide any person with information about the Foundation where the provision of that information is prohibited under the Foundation’s Char...

374.text: (3) If Article 53(2) applies, the person making the request may apply to the Court under Article 39 for an order authorising or requiring the provision of the requested information, in which case it m...

375.text: (a) the Foundation is carrying out its objects; (b) the Council of the Foundation is carrying out its functions; (c) the property of the Foundation is being properly administered; and (d) the Foundati...

376.text: (4) Article 53(2) does not affect any other obligation of a Foundation to supply information about the Foundation under this Law or the power of DIFCA, the Registrar or the DFSA to obtain information ...

378.text: (1) No action or proceeding whether pursuant to this Law or at common law or in equity:

379.text: (a) to set aside the establishment of a Foundation; (b) to set aside any disposition to any Foundation; or (c) for any order under this Part,

380.text: shall be commenced, unless such action or proceeding is commenced in the Court before the expiration of three (3) years from:

381.text: (i) the date of the establishment of the Foundation that is sought to be set aside; (ii) the disposition to the Foundation that is sought to be set aside; or (iii) the right to commence the action or ...

382.text: (2) No action or proceeding whether pursuant to this Law or at common law or in equity shall be commenced by any person:

383.text:
(a) claiming to have had an interest in property before that property was settled upon or disposed to a Foundation; and   
(b) seeking to derive a legal or equitable interest in that property,

384.text: unless such action or proceeding is commenced in the Court before the expiration of three (3) years from the date that the property referred to in paragraphs (a) and (b) was disposed to a Foundation.

385.text: PART 8: CONTINUATION AND RECOGNISED FOREIGN FOUNDATIONS

386.text: 54. Continuation of a Foreign Foundation in the DIFC

387.text: (1) A Foreign Foundation may, if not prohibited in terms of its founding Documents or under the laws of the jurisdiction under which it is organised, apply to the Registrar for a certificate of contin...

388.text: (2) An application made under Article 54(1) shall be in such form as may be prescribed by the Registrar and be accompanied by a Charter of Continuance, and, if the application does not propose the app...

389.text: (3) A Charter of Continuance may, without so stating in the Document, effect any amendment to the organisational instruments of the Foreign Foundation that applies for continuance under this Article, ...

390.text: (a) is authorised in accordance with the law applicable to the Foreign Foundation before continuance under this Law; and (b) is an amendment that a Foundation is entitled to make under this Law.

391.text: 55. Charter of Continuance for Foreign Foundations

392.text: (1) The Charter of Continuance of a Foreign Foundation shall be written in the English language.

393.text: (2) The Charter of Continuance of a Foreign Foundation shall:

394.text:
(a) be signed by all of the members of the Council or substantially equivalent officers of the Foreign Foundation;   
(b) state the name of the Foreign Foundation and the name under which it will be c...

397.text: (2) On the date shown in the certificate of continuance:

398.text:
(a) the Foreign Foundation shall become a Foundation in the DIFC to which this Law shall apply as if it had been established under this Law; and   
(b) the Charter of Continuance shall become the Char...

401.text: (a) the property of the Foreign Foundation becomes the property of the Foundation;

404.text: (1) Where a Foreign Foundation is registered as a Foundation under this Part, the Foundation shall, as soon as possible (but not exceeding a period of three (3) months), file with the Registrar any ce...

405.text: (2) If the Court is satisfied that:

406.text:

408.text: (i) the Foundation, any Person with Sufficient Interest or a creditor; or (ii) the Registrar,

409.text: make an order for the removal of the Foundation’s name from the Register.

410.text: (3) An order under Article 58(2) may be made subject to such terms and conditions and such penalty as the Court thinks fit.

415.text: 60. Conditions applicable to a Foundation leaving the DIFC

416.text: A Foundation may not apply for continuance in another jurisdiction, nor may it be continued under the laws of another jurisdiction, as an entity established in that other jurisdiction pursuant to Arti...

422.text: (1) A Foreign Foundation which wishes to conduct operations within the DIFC may apply for a Licence to be a Recognised Foreign Foundation in the DIFC for the purposes of this Law, in accordance with A...

423.text: 63. Continuation of Company as a DIFC Foundation

424.text: (1) A Company incorporated under the Companies Law may, if authorized by a unanimous resolution of all of its shareholders, apply to the Registrar for a certificate of continuance under this Law.

425.text: (2) An application made under Article 63(1) shall be in such form as may be prescribed by the Registrar and be accompanied by a Charter of Continuance, and, if the application does not propose the app...

426.text: (3) A Charter of Continuance and proposed By-laws (if any) shall effect any amendment to the memorandum and Articles of Association of the Company that applies for continuance under this Article neces...

427.text: 64. Charter of Continuance of a Company

428.text: (1) The Charter of Continuance of a Company shall be written in the English language.

429.text: (2) The Charter of Continuance of a Company shall:

430.text: (a) be signed by all of the directors of the Company;

433.text: (1) Upon receipt of the Charter of Continuance and, if applicable, the proposed By-laws, the Registrar shall if he is satisfied that Articles 63 and 64 have been complied with, issue a certificate of ...

435.text:

(b) the Charter of Continuance shall become the Charter thereof in ...

436.text: 66. Preservation of a Company as a Foundation

437.text: When a Company is continued as a Foundation in the DIFC under this Law:

439.text: 66A. Continuation of DIFC Foundation as a Company

440.text: (1) A DIFC Foundation registered pursuant to this Law may, if it is:

441.text: (a) permitted under its Charter and By-laws to do so; and (b) authorised by a unanimous resolution of its Council Members, apply to the Registrar for a certificate of continuance under the Companies L...

442.text: (2) An application made under Article 66A(1) shall be in such form as may be prescribed by the Registrar and be accompanied by Articles of Continuation that comply with Articles 11(1) and 11(2) of the...

443.text: (3) The Articles of Continuation shall make any amendments to the Charter and By-laws of the DIFC Foundation necessary to make the Articles of Continuation conform to the Companies Law and any other r...

444.text: 66B. Articles of Continuation of a Company

445.text: (1) The Articles of Continuation of a DIFC Foundation continuing under the Companies Law shall be written in the English language.

448.text: 66C. Certificate of continuation of a DIFC Foundation

449.text: (1) Upon receipt of the Articles of Continuation the Registrar shall if he is satisfied that Articles 66A and 66B have been complied with, issue a certificate of continuance in respect of the Company,...

450.text: (2) On the date shown in the certificate of continuance:

451.text:
(a) the DIFC Foundation shall become a Company to which the Companies Law shall apply as if it had been established under the Companies Law;   
(b) the Articles of Continuation shall become the Articl...

452.text: 66D. Preservation of a DIFC Foundation as a Company

453.text: When a DIFC Foundation is continued as a Company in the DIFC under the Companies Law:

461.text: (1) A Foundation may, upon application, by an order of the Court be dissolved if the Court is of the opinion that:

(4) Where the Court orders that a Foundation be dissolved under this Article, the Court may a...

(a) there is no person entitled to receive the remaining property of the Foundation as provided in Article 69(1); or

470.text: 70. Application of insolvency rules in winding up

471.text: (1) Unless the Regulations provide otherwise, in the winding up of an insolvent Foundation the same rules concerning rights of creditors, proof of debts, valuation of liabilities and preferential paym...

472.text: (2) The Regulations may provide for the manner in which the Insolvency Law will apply to the winding up of insolvent Foundations.

474.text: (1) If the Registrar has reason to believe that:

475.text: (a) a Foundation is acting in contravention of this Law and the Regulations; or (b) it is prejudicial to the interest of the DIFC for a Foundation to remain on the Register, he may give notice to the ...

476.text: (2) If by the end of the three (3) month period, the Registrar has not received from the Foundation an objection to the striking off of the Foundation from the Register or from any other party suffici...

477.text: (3) When a Foundation has been struck off the Register under Article 71(2), the Foundation or its liquidator may apply to have the name of the Foundation reinstated on the Register, provided such appl...

478.text: (4) Where an application is made under Article 71(3) and upon payment of all outstanding fees and penalties to the Registrar, the Registrar may reinstate the Foundation on the Register.

479.text: (5) Where the Foundation is reinstated on the Register under Article 71(4), the Foundation is deemed to have continued in existence as if it had not been dissolved or struck off the Register.

480.text: Upon dissolution of a Foundation under this Article, its outstanding property shall become the property of the DIFCA, provided that if it is reinstated to the Register such property shall once again b...

482.text: The Registrar shall publish the dissolution of a Foundation on the website of the DIFC.
